title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself poses minimal direct risk, but its widespread adoption could lead to potential misuse by attackers to identify and exploit unpatched vulnerabilities in target systems.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool in a controlled environment to ensure it aligns with organizational security policies and does not introduce additional risks.
Educate development and security teams on the responsible use of such tools to avoid exposing sensitive information or creating attack vectors.
Monitor for any updates or vulnerabilities in the tool itself, as attackers may target popular open-source security tools.

title: pyLoad: Unprotected storage_folder enables arbitrary file write to Flask session store and code execution (Incomplete fix for CVE-2026-33509)
description:
## Summary

The fix for CVE-2026-33509 (GHSA-r7mc-x6x7-cqxx) added an `ADMIN_ONLY_OPTIONS` set to block non-admin users from modifying security-critical config options. The `storage_folder` option is not in this set and passes the existing path restriction because the Flask session directory is outside both PKGDIR and userdir. A user with SETTINGS and ADD permissions can redirect downloads to the Flask filesystem session store, plant a malicious pickle payload as a predictable session file, and

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-35464.

title: pyLoad: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command
description:
### Summary

The `ADMIN_ONLY_OPTIONS` protection mechanism restricts security-critical configuration values (reconnect scripts, SSL certs, proxy credentials) to admin-only access. However, this protection is **only applied to core config options**, not to plugin config options. The `AntiVirus` plugin stores an executable path (`avfile`) in its config, which is passed directly to `subprocess.Popen()`. A non-admin user with SETTINGS permission can change this path to achieve remote code execution.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows non-admin users with SETTINGS permissions to execute arbitrary code by modifying the executable path in the AntiVirus plugin configuration. This could lead to unauthorized access, data breaches, or system compromise.
recommended_actions: Immediately restrict SETTINGS permissions to trusted users only and review current user permissions.
Apply patches or updates provided by the pyLoad development team to address the vulnerability.
Implement monitoring and logging to detect any unauthorized changes to plugin configurations.

title: pyLoad: SSRF filter bypass via HTTP redirect in BaseDownloader (Incomplete fix for CVE-2026-33992)
description:
## Summary

The fix for CVE-2026-33992 (GHSA-m74m-f7cr-432x) added IP validation to `BaseDownloader.download()` that checks the hostname of the initial download URL. However, pycurl is configured with `FOLLOWLOCATION=1` and `MAXREDIRS=10`, causing it to automatically follow HTTP redirects. Redirect targets are never validated against the SSRF filter.

An authenticated user with ADD permission can bypass the SSRF fix by submitting a URL that redirects to an internal address.

## Root Cause

The S

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-35459.

title: web3.py: SSRF via CCIP Read (EIP-3668) OffchainLookup URL handling
description:
## Summary

web3.py implements CCIP Read / `OffchainLookup` (EIP-3668) by performing HTTP requests to URLs supplied by smart contracts in `offchain_lookup_payload["urls"]`. The implementation uses these contract-supplied URLs directly (after `{sender}` / `{data}` template substitution) without any destination validation:

- No restriction to `https://` (and no opt-in gate for `http://`)
- No hostname or IP allowlist
- No blocking of private/reserved IP ranges (loopback, link-local, RFC1918)
- No

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity.

title: libp2p-rendezvous: Unbounded rendezvous DISCOVER cookies enable remote memory exhaustion
description:
### Summary
The rendezvous server stores pagination cookies without bounds. An unauthenticated peer can repeatedly issue `DISCOVER` requests and force unbounded memory growth.

### Details

Pagination state is stored in:

```rs
HashMap<Cookie, HashSet<RegistrationId>>
```

On `Message::Discover`:

```
remote peer
→ DISCOVER
→ handle_request
→ registrations.get(...)
→ new cookie generated
→ cookie inserted into Registrations::cookies
```

There is **no upper bound or eviction policy**, so repeate

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthenticated peers to issue repeated DISCOVER requests, causing unbounded memory growth on the rendezvous server. This could lead to memory exhaustion, denial of service, and potential server crashes.
recommended_actions: Implement an upper bound or eviction policy for pagination cookies in the HashMap to limit memory usage.
Introduce rate limiting or authentication mechanisms to prevent abuse of the DISCOVER request functionality.
Conduct a thorough code review and testing to ensure the fix does not introduce new vulnerabilities.

title: libp2p-rendezvous: Unlimited namespace registrations per peer enables OOM DoS on rendezvous servers
description:
### Summary

The`libp2p-rendezvous` server has no limit on how many namespaces a single peer can register. A malicious peer can repeatedly register unique namespaces in a loop, and the server accepts the requests, allocating memory for each registration without pushback. If an attacker continues submitting malicous requests for long enough, (or with multiple sybil peers) the server process crashes due to OOM.

No auth is required; therefore, any peer on the network can do this.



### Details

t

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-35405.

title: The Xpro Addons — 140+ Widgets for Elementor plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross...
description: The Xpro Addons — 140+ Widgets for Elementor plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the Icon Box widget in versions up to, and including, 1.4.24 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with contributor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-2949.

title: The Gutenverse – Ultimate WordPress FSE Blocks Addons & Ecosystem plugin for WordPress is...
description: The Gutenverse – Ultimate WordPress FSE Blocks Addons & Ecosystem plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the 'imageLoad' parameter in versions up to, and including, 3.4.6 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with contributor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated attackers with contributor-level access or higher to inject malicious scripts into WordPress pages, potentially leading to data theft, session hijacking, or defacement of the site. This could compromise the integrity and security of affected websites and their users.
recommended_actions: Update the Gutenverse plugin to a patched version as soon as it becomes available or disable the plugin temporarily if no update is available.
Restrict contributor-level access to only trusted users and review user permissions to minimize potential attack vectors.
Implement a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious scripts targeting this vulnerability.

title: OpenClaw: Gemini OAuth exposed the PKCE verifier through the OAuth state parameter
description:
## Summary

Before OpenClaw 2026.4.2, the Gemini OAuth flow reused the PKCE verifier as the OAuth `state` value. Because the provider reflected `state` back in the redirect URL, the verifier could be exposed alongside the authorization code.

## Impact

Anyone who could capture the redirect URL could learn both the authorization code and the PKCE verifier, defeating PKCE's interception protection for that flow and enabling token redemption.

## Affected Packages / Versions

- Package: `openclaw`

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Code Extension Marketplace: Zip Slip Path Traversal
description:
# Zip Slip Path Traversal in coder/code-marketplace

## Summary

A Zip Slip (CWE-22) vulnerability in `coder/code-marketplace` ≤ v2.4.1 allowed a malicious VSIX file to write arbitrary files outside the extension directory. `ExtractZip` passed raw zip entry names to a callback that wrote files via `filepath.Join` with no boundary check; `filepath.Join` resolved `..` components but did not prevent the result from escaping the base path.


## Root Cause

`ExtractZip` passed the raw, attacker-contr

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-35454.

title: defu: Prototype pollution via `__proto__` key in defaults argument
description:
### Impact

Applications that pass unsanitized user input (e.g. parsed JSON request bodies, database records, or config files from untrusted sources) as the first argument to `defu()` are vulnerable to prototype pollution.

A crafted payload containing a `__proto__` key can override intended default values in the merged result:

```js
import { defu } from 'defu'

const userInput = JSON.parse('{"__proto__":{"isAdmin":true}}')
const config = defu(userInput, { isAdmin: false })

config.isAdmin // t

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to exploit prototype pollution by injecting malicious payloads via unsanitized user input. This can lead to unauthorized access, privilege escalation, or application behavior manipulation, potentially compromising sensitive data and system integrity.
recommended_actions: Sanitize all user inputs before passing them to the `defu()` function to prevent malicious payloads from being processed.
Update to a patched version of the `defu` library that addresses this vulnerability, if available.
Implement runtime validation to detect and block objects containing `__proto__` keys or other prototype pollution vectors.

title: AVideo: Unauthenticated Information Disclosure via Missing Auth on CloneSite client.log.php
description:
## Summary

The `plugin/CloneSite/client.log.php` endpoint serves the clone operation log file without any authentication. Every other endpoint in the CloneSite plugin directory enforces `User::isAdmin()`. The log contains internal filesystem paths, remote server URLs, and SSH connection metadata.

## Details

The entire file at `plugin/CloneSite/client.log.php`:

```php
<?php
include '../../videos/cache/clones/client.log';
```

No authentication check. The log file is populated by `cloneClient.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-35452.

title: AVideo: Unauthenticated FFmpeg Remote Server Status Disclosure via check.ffmpeg.json.php
description:
## Summary

The `plugin/API/check.ffmpeg.json.php` endpoint probes the FFmpeg remote server configuration and returns connectivity status without any authentication. All sibling FFmpeg management endpoints (`kill.ffmpeg.json.php`, `list.ffmpeg.json.php`, `ffmpeg.php`) require `User::isAdmin()`.

## Details

The entire file at `plugin/API/check.ffmpeg.json.php`:

```php
<?php
$configFile = __DIR__.'/../../videos/configuration.php';
require_once $configFile;
header('Content-Type: application/json'

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The unauthenticated endpoint allows attackers to probe FFmpeg server configurations and connectivity status, potentially exposing sensitive server information. While this does not directly allow for remote code execution, it can aid attackers in reconnaissance and planning further attacks.
recommended_actions: Restrict access to the `check.ffmpeg.json.php` endpoint by implementing authentication or IP whitelisting.
Review and update the FFmpeg plugin to ensure all endpoints require proper authentication, similar to other FFmpeg management endpoints.
Monitor server logs for unusual activity targeting the `check.ffmpeg.json.php` endpoint and take appropriate action if suspicious behavior is detected.

title: AVideo: Unauthenticated Information Disclosure via Disabled CLI Guard in install/test.php

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-35449.

title: AVideo: Unauthenticated Access to Payment Order Data via BlockonomicsYPT check.php
description:
## Summary

The BlockonomicsYPT plugin's `check.php` endpoint returns payment order data for any Bitcoin address without requiring authentication. The endpoint was designed as an AJAX polling helper for the authenticated `invoice.php` page, but it performs no access control checks of its own. Since Bitcoin addresses are publicly visible on the blockchain, an attacker can query payment records for any address used on the platform.

## Details

In `plugin/BlockonomicsYPT/check.php` at lines 20-30,

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthenticated access to payment order data associated with Bitcoin addresses, potentially exposing sensitive transaction details. While the data exposed is limited to payment records and does not directly compromise user credentials or private keys, it could lead to privacy violations, profiling of users, or targeted phishing attacks.
recommended_actions: Implement proper access control checks in the `check.php` endpoint to ensure only authenticated users can query payment order data.
Conduct a security review of all AJAX endpoints to identify and remediate similar vulnerabilities.
Notify affected users about the potential exposure of their payment order data and recommend monitoring for suspicious activity.

title: LightRAG: Hardcoded JWT Signing Secret Allows Authentication Bypass
description:
Summary:
The file lightrag/api/config.py (line 397) uses a default JWT secret "lightrag-jwt-default-secret" when the TOKEN_SECRET environment variable is not set. The AuthHandler in lightrag/api/auth.py (lines 24-25) uses this secret to sign and verify tokens. An unauthenticated attacker can forge valid JWT tokens using the publicly known default secret and gain access to any protected endpoint.

Reproduction:
1. Install LightRAG v1.4.10 with AUTH_ACCOUNTS configured but no TOKEN_SECRET set
2. U

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The use of a hardcoded JWT signing secret allows attackers to bypass authentication mechanisms by forging valid JWT tokens. This could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive data, privilege escalation, and potential compromise of the entire application or system.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the application to use a strong, unique TOKEN_SECRET environment variable for JWT signing and verification. Ensure this secret is not hardcoded and is stored securely.
Audit all deployments of LightRAG v1.4.10 to ensure the TOKEN_SECRET is properly configured. If not, set a secure secret and rotate any potentially compromised tokens.
Encourage all users to update to a patched version of LightRAG (if available) or apply a hotfix to remove the hardcoded secret and enforce secure configurations.

title: Directus: Authenticated Users Can Extract Concealed Fields via Aggregate Queries
description:
### Summary

Aggregate functions (`min`, `max`) applied to fields with the `conceal` special type incorrectly return raw database values instead of the masked placeholder. When combined with `groupBy`, any authenticated user with read access to the affected collection can extract concealed field values, including static API tokens and two-factor authentication secrets from `directus_users`.

### Details

Fields marked with `conceal` are protected by payload processing logic that replaces real va

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-35442.

title: Directus: Unauthenticated Denial of Service via GraphQL Alias Amplification of Expensive Health Check Resolver
description:
## Summary

The GraphQL specification permits a single query to repeat the same field multiple times using aliases, with each alias resolved independently by default. Directus did not deduplicate resolver invocations within a single request, meaning each alias triggered a full, independent execution of the underlying resolver.

The health check resolver ran all backend checks (database connectivity, cache, storage writes, and SMTP verification) on every invocation. Combined with unauthenticated

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Directus: GraphQL Alias Amplification Denial of Service Due to Missing Query Cost/Complexity Limits
description:
### Summary

Directus' GraphQL endpoints (`/graphql` and `/graphql/system`) did not deduplicate resolver invocations within a single request. An authenticated user could exploit GraphQL aliasing to repeat an expensive relational query many times in a single request, forcing the server to execute a large number of independent complex database queries concurrently, multiplying database load linearly with the number of aliases. The existing token limit on GraphQL queries still permitted enough alia

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-35441.

title: Directus: Sensitive fields exposed in revision history
description:
### Summary

Directus stores revision records (in `directus_revisions`) whenever items are created or updated. Due to the revision snapshot code not consistently calling the `prepareDelta` sanitization pipeline, sensitive fields (including user tokens, two-factor authentication secrets, external auth identifiers, auth data, stored credentials, and AI provider API keys) could be stored in plaintext within revision records.

Additionally, the same sensitive fields were missing from the redaction l

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Sensitive information, including user tokens, authentication secrets, and API keys, could be exposed in plaintext within revision records. This increases the risk of unauthorized access, data breaches, and potential exploitation of critical systems.
recommended_actions: Update Directus to the latest version as soon as a patch addressing this vulnerability is released.
Review and sanitize existing revision records in the `directus_revisions` table to ensure no sensitive data is stored in plaintext.
Implement additional access controls and monitoring on the database to detect and prevent unauthorized access to sensitive fields.

title: CVE-2016-20040 - TiEmu 3.03-nogdb+dfsg-3 contains a buffer overflow vulnerability in the ROM parameter handling that
description: TiEmu 3.03-nogdb+dfsg-3 contains a buffer overflow vulnerability in the ROM parameter handling that allows local attackers to crash the application or execute arbitrary code. Attackers can supply an oversized ROM parameter to the tiemu command-line interface to overflow the stack buffer and overwrite the instruction pointer with malicious addresses.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The buffer overflow vulnerability in TiEmu 3.03-nogdb+dfsg-3 allows local attackers to crash the application or execute arbitrary code, potentially leading to unauthorized system access, data corruption, or further exploitation of the affected system.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to a patched or newer version of TiEmu if available, or consider using an alternative emulator without known vulnerabilities.
Restrict local access to the vulnerable system to only trusted users and implement strict access controls.
Monitor systems for unusual activity and implement intrusion detection mechanisms to identify potential exploitation attempts.

title: CVE-2016-20039 - Multi Emulator Super System 0.154-3.1 contains a buffer overflow vulnerability in the gamma paramete
description: Multi Emulator Super System 0.154-3.1 contains a buffer overflow vulnerability in the gamma parameter handling that allows local attackers to crash the application or execute arbitrary code. Attackers can supply an oversized gamma parameter value to overflow the stack buffer and overwrite the instruction pointer with a controlled address to achieve code execution.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2016-20039.

title: CVE-2016-20038 - yTree 1.94-1.1 contains a stack-based buffer overflow vulnerability that allows local attackers to e
description: yTree 1.94-1.1 contains a stack-based buffer overflow vulnerability that allows local attackers to execute arbitrary code by supplying an excessively long argument to the application. Attackers can craft a malicious command-line argument containing shellcode and a return address to overwrite the stack and execute code in the application context.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2016-20038.

title: CVE-2016-20037 - xwpe 1.5.30a-2.1 and prior contains a stack-based buffer overflow vulnerability that allows local at
description: xwpe 1.5.30a-2.1 and prior contains a stack-based buffer overflow vulnerability that allows local attackers to execute arbitrary code by supplying overly long input strings that exceed buffer boundaries. Attackers can craft malicious command-line arguments with 262 bytes of junk data followed by shellcode to overwrite the instruction pointer and achieve code execution or denial of service.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2016-20037.

title: CVE-2026-4995 - A vulnerability was determined in wandb OpenUI up to 1.0. Affected by this vulnerability is an unkno
description: A vulnerability was determined in wandb OpenUI up to 1.0. Affected by this vulnerability is an unknown functionality of the file frontend/public/annotator/index.html of the component Window Message Event Handler. This manipulation causes cross site scripting. The attack can be initiated remotely. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4995.

title: CVE-2025-9497 - Use of Hard-coded Credentials vulnerability in Microchip Time Provider 4100 allows Malicious Manual
description: Use of Hard-coded Credentials vulnerability in Microchip Time Provider 4100 allows Malicious Manual Software Update.This issue affects Time Provider 4100: before 2.5.0.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2025-9497.

title: CVE-2026-4994 - A vulnerability was found in wandb OpenUI up to 1.0/3.5-turb. Affected is the function generic_excep
description: A vulnerability was found in wandb OpenUI up to 1.0/3.5-turb. Affected is the function generic_exception_handler of the file backend/openui/server.py of the component APIStatusError Handler. The manipulation of the argument key results in information exposure through error message. Access to the local network is required for this attack. The exploit has been made public and could be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4994.

title: CVE-2026-4993 - A vulnerability has been found in wandb OpenUI up to 0.0.0.0/1.0. This impacts an unknown function o
description: A vulnerability has been found in wandb OpenUI up to 0.0.0.0/1.0. This impacts an unknown function of the file backend/openui/config.py. The manipulation of the argument LITELLM_MASTER_KEY leads to hard-coded credentials. An attack has to be approached locally. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4993.

title: CVE-2026-2442 - The Page Builder: Pagelayer – Drag and Drop website builder plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Im
description: The Page Builder: Pagelayer – Drag and Drop website builder plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Improper Neutralization of CRLF Sequences ('CRLF Injection') in all versions up to, and including, 2.0.7. This is due to the contact form handler performing placeholder substitution on attacker-controlled form fields and then passing the resulting values into email headers without removing CR/LF characters. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to inject arbitrary email headers (for example Bcc / Cc) and abuse form email delivery via the 'email' parameter granted they can target a contact form configured to use placeholders in mail template headers.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-2442.

title:
CVE-2026-23399 - In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

nf_tables: nft_dynset: fix poss
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

nf_tables: nft_dynset: fix possible stateful expression memleak in error path

If cloning the second stateful expression in the element via GFP_ATOMIC
fails, then the first stateful expression remains in place without being
released.

   unreferenced object (percpu) 0x607b97e9cab8 (size 16):
     comm "softirq", pid 0, jiffies 4294931867
     hex dump (first 16 bytes on cpu 3):
       00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00
     backtrace (crc 0):
       pcpu_alloc_noprof+0x453/0xd80
       nft_counter_clone+0x9c/0x190 [nf_tables]
       nft_expr_clone+0x8f/0x1b0 [nf_tables]
       nft_dynset_new+0x2cb/0x5f0 [nf_tables]
       nft_rhash_update+0x236/0x11c0 [nf_tables]
       nft_dynset_eval+0x11f/0x670 [nf_tables]
       nft_do_chain+0x253/0x1700 [nf_tables]
       nft_do_chain_ipv4+0x18d/0x270 [nf_tables]
       nf_hook_slow+0xaa/0x1e0
       ip_local_deliver+0x209/0x330

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-23399.

title: CVE-2026-1307 - The Ninja Forms - The Contact Form Builder That Grows With You plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to
description: The Ninja Forms - The Contact Form Builder That Grows With You plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Sensitive Information Exposure in all versions up to, and including, 3.14.1 via a callback function for the admin_enqueue_scripts action handler in blocks/bootstrap.php. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Contributor-level access and above, to gain access to an authorization token to view form submissions for arbitrary forms, which could potentially contain sensitive information.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-1307.

title: CVE-2025-15445 - The Restaurant Cafeteria WordPress theme through 0.4.6 exposes insecure admin-ajax actions without n
description: The Restaurant Cafeteria WordPress theme through 0.4.6 exposes insecure admin-ajax actions without nonce or capability checks, allowing any logged-in user, like subscriber, to perform privileged operations. An attacker can install and activate a  from a user-supplied URL, leading to arbitrary PHP code execution, and also import demo content that rewrites site configuration, including Restaurant Cafeteria WordPress theme through 0.4.6_mods, pages, menus, and front page settings.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2025-15445.

title: CVE-2025-12886 - The Oxygen Theme theme for WordPress is vulnerable to Server-Side Request Forgery in all versions up
description: The Oxygen Theme theme for WordPress is vulnerable to Server-Side Request Forgery in all versions up to, and including, 6.0.8 via the laborator_calc_route AJAX action. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to make web requests to arbitrary locations originating from the web application and can be used to query and modify information from internal services.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2025-12886.

title: CVE-2026-4987 - The SureForms – Contact Form, Payment Form & Other Custom Form Builder plugin for WordPress is vulne
description: The SureForms – Contact Form, Payment Form & Other Custom Form Builder plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Payment Amount Bypass in all versions up to, and including, 2.5.2. This is due to the create_payment_intent() function performing a payment validation solely based on the value of a user-controlled parameter. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to bypass configured form payment-amount validation and create underpriced payment/subscription intents by setting form_id to 0.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4987.

title: CVE-2026-1679 - The eswifi socket offload driver copies user-provided payloads into a fixed buffer without checking
description: The eswifi socket offload driver copies user-provided payloads into a fixed buffer without checking available space; oversized sends overflow `eswifi->buf`, corrupting kernel memory (CWE-120). Exploit requires local code that can call the socket send API; no remote attacker can reach it directly.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-1679.
